---
title: "Week 9: Model Building & Diagnostics"
date: "2026-10-12"
---

## Overview

This week combines two essential topics in applied regression: model building strategies and regression diagnostics. You will learn how to approach variable selection using both confirmatory and exploratory strategies, understand information criteria for model comparison, and then apply a suite of diagnostic tools to evaluate model assumptions. Residual plots, Q-Q plots, and influence measures such as Cook's distance and DFBETAS will help you identify problematic observations and assess whether your model provides a reliable description of the data.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Distinguish between confirmatory and exploratory approaches to model building
- Apply forward selection, backward elimination, and stepwise procedures for variable selection
- Use AIC and BIC to compare competing models and justify model choice
- Construct and interpret residual plots and Q-Q plots to assess model assumptions
- Calculate and interpret Cook's distance, DFBETAS, and leverage values to identify influential observations
- Evaluate the practical impact of influential observations on regression conclusions

## Readings

- {{< fa book-open >}} **Required**: [Chapter 7: Model Building]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/07-model-building.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa book-open >}} **Required**: [Chapter 8: Model Diagnostics]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/08-model-diagnostics.html){target="_blank"}

## R Lab

- {{< fa laptop-code >}} [Lab 08: Model Building and Diagnostics]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}/labs/lab08-diagnostics.html){target="_blank"}

## Project

- {{< fa file-circle-check >}} [Project 1 Draft](/assignment/project-1-draft.qmd) — Due this week

## Quiz

- {{< fa circle-question >}} **Quiz 4** — Covers Weeks 7--9 material

## Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Forward selection** | A stepwise procedure that begins with no predictors and adds them one at a time based on statistical criteria |
| **Backward elimination** | A stepwise procedure that begins with all candidate predictors and removes them one at a time |
| **AIC** | Akaike Information Criterion; balances model fit and complexity, favoring parsimony |
| **BIC** | Bayesian Information Criterion; similar to AIC but applies a stronger penalty for additional parameters |
| **Residual plot** | A scatterplot of residuals against fitted values used to check linearity and constant variance assumptions |
| **Q-Q plot** | A quantile-quantile plot comparing residual quantiles to theoretical normal quantiles to assess normality |
| **Cook's distance** | A summary measure of how much all fitted values change when a single observation is deleted |
| **DFBETAS** | A measure of how much each regression coefficient changes when a single observation is removed |
| **Leverage** | A measure of how far an observation's predictor values are from the mean of all predictor values |
| **Influential observation** | An observation whose removal substantially changes the fitted model or its conclusions |
